Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A long detailed description of a property management company property onboarding process Task Generate a list of targeted open ended questions to ask the interviewee Goal Clarify and improve understanding of the process uncover missing details decision criteria roles timing exceptions Constraint Do not ask for SQL queries or technical implementation details Focus on conceptual understanding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Source Text Process Deconstruction Step 1 Initiation Documentation Landlord contacts regional office Coordinator reviews docs deeds compliance inspections Missing docs request updates Verified enter into DB sq ft units location amenities price Step 2 Manager Assignment Assign dedicated PM Criteria workload geo experience track record Intro call meeting expectations policies pets maintenance payment Step 3 Inspection Marketing Schedule photographer inspector Inspector finds issues PM works with landlord contractors Pass inspection Digital marketing team listings brochures pricing models Step 4 Activation Tenant Screening Activate in portal PM Leasing Agent screens tenants apps credit employment references Multiple tenants negotiation discounts prioritization Choose tenant finalize lease deposit move in Step 5 QA Audit Compliance officer fair housing Financial analyst pricing profitability IT data integration General Multiple decision points stakeholders exceptions insurance custom clauses communication tracking email CRM PM tools

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Identify Gaps Areas for Clarification Brainstorming Triggers What exactly triggers the start Is it a form a call an email SLAs Timing How long does each step take Are there deadlines Decision Logic How is workload measured for PM assignment Who makes the final call on pricing Exceptions What happens if a landlord refuses repairs What if no tenant is found Roles Who is the Property Coordinator Is this a distinct role from the PM What if the PM is unavailable Systems You mentioned CRM PM tools but how do hand offs happen Is it manual or automated Feedback Loops If an audit fails does the process restart Metrics How do they measure success of onboarding

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Drafting Questions Iterative Refinement Draft 1 Too specific technical What database do you use for the property details Refinement 1 Conceptual How is the property data validated before it enters the central database to ensure accuracy Draft 2 Yes No Do you have a deadline for the inspection Refinement 2 Open ended What are the expected timeframes between the initial contact and the property being activated for rent Draft 3 Role clarity Who assigns the manager Refinement 3 Decision criteria Can you walk me through the decision making process for assigning a property manager Is this automated or a manual management decision Draft 4 Exceptions What if the inspection fails Refinement 4 Exception handling You mentioned inspectors might find issues What is the protocol if a landlord is unwilling or unable to make the required repairs Does the onboarding stop there Draft 5 Audit What happens if the compliance officer says no Refinement 5 Process Flow Regarding the final quality checks if the compliance officer or financial analyst flags an issue does the process revert to a previous stage or is there a remediation path

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Categorizing and Polishing the Questions To make the output structured and professional Ill group the questions by logical themes Initiation Assignment Inspection Marketing Tenant Selection Audit Exceptions This helps the interviewee follow the flow Category 1 Initiation Documentation Focus on the Gatekeeping aspect Category 2 Resource Allocation Focus on the PM assignment logic Category 3 Property Readiness Focus on the inspection repair loop Category 4 Tenant Acquisition Focus on the screening and negotiation complexity Category 5 Governance Exceptions Focus on the audits and edge cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Constraints Are they open ended Yes Do they ask for SQL Code No Do they cover missing details criteria roles timing exceptions Yes

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Final Output Generation This matches the provided good response Self Correction during generation Make sure I dont just repeat what they said Ask how they know who decides and what if Ensure the tone is inquisitive and professional instead of Who does the pricing ask How is the final rental price determined and approved

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Final Polish Check for clarity and flow The resulting list looks solid
